steps.title: Set up
steps.content: Right triangle ABC has a right angle at B, hypotenuse $AC = 90$, and $\tan(\angle C) = \frac{3}{4}$. Since $\tan(C) = \frac{\text{opposite}}{\text{adjacent}} = \frac{AB}{BC}$, write $AB = 3m$ and $BC = 4m$.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — $\frac{35}{16}$: This is far too small for a side in a triangle with hypotenuse 90. ✗
  • Choice B — $\frac{15}{4}$: Also far too small for a triangle with hypotenuse 90. ✗
  • Choice C — $54$: $AB = 3m = 3(18) = 54$. Check: $54^2 + 72^2 = 2916 + 5184 = 8100 = 90^2$. ✓
  • Choice D — $72$: That would be $BC$, not $AB$. $BC = 4m = 72$, but the question asks for $AB$. ✗
That confirms C is correct.
